evals.prompt: I downloaded a research paper as a PDF (smith_2023_tmb.pdf) and I want it as clean Markdown so I can paste it into an LLM prompt without wasting tokens on layout junk. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-markitdown. Recommends Microsoft MarkItDown to convert the PDF to token-efficient Markdown, e.g. the markitdown CLI (`markitdown smith_2023_tmb.pdf -o smith_2023_tmb.md`) or the Python API (`from markitdown import MarkItDown; MarkItDown().convert(...)`, reading `result.text_content`). May note pip install 'markitdown[pdf]' for PDF support.

assertions.value: Mentions converting the PDF to Markdown via the markitdown CLI or the MarkItDown Python API.

evals.prompt: I have a folder of mixed files for a literature review — a bunch of PDFs, some DOCX, a couple of PPTX decks and an XLSX of extracted data. Can I batch-convert all of them to Markdown in one pass?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-markitdown. Explains that MarkItDown handles all these formats (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX) and shows a batch loop (e.g. iterating files with pathlib glob and calling MarkItDown().convert on each, writing one .md per file). May mention installing 'markitdown[all]' for full format support.

assertions.value: Describes batch-converting multiple document formats (DOCX/PPTX/XLSX/PDF) to Markdown, ideally with a loop over the files.

evals.prompt: I have a scanned figure saved as a PNG with embedded text labels, plus a JPEG of a handwritten-ish chart. I need the text pulled out of these images into Markdown — does this tool do OCR?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-markitdown. Confirms MarkItDown extracts text from images (JPEG/PNG) via OCR and also reads EXIF metadata, converting to Markdown. May note that OCR requires tesseract installed (e.g. `brew install tesseract`) and that AI-enhanced image descriptions are optionally available via an LLM client.

assertions.value: Confirms image OCR text extraction to Markdown and mentions a dependency such as tesseract.

evals.prompt: There's a conference talk on YouTube I want the transcript of as Markdown text so I can quote it in my notes. Can I just hand the video URL to a converter?
evals.expected_output: Triggers alterlab-markitdown. Explains MarkItDown can fetch a YouTube video's transcript directly from the URL (e.g. MarkItDown().convert('https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...')) and return it as Markdown. May mention the [youtube-transcription] optional dependency.

assertions.value: Describes passing the YouTube URL to MarkItDown to obtain the transcript as Markdown.

evals.prompt: I want to ingest a stack of PDFs into a self-hosted NotebookLM-style workspace, then chat with them and generate a multi-speaker podcast from my sources. How do I set that up?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger alterlab-markitdown — this is not a one-shot file-to-Markdown conversion task. Ingesting sources into a self-hosted research notebook with context-aware chat and podcast generation is alterlab-open-notebook's territory (Open Notebook REST API, /sources, /chat, /podcasts). Defer to alterlab-open-notebook.
